name: Sid Weiss
abstract: Sid Weiss (April 30, 1914 – March 30, 1994) was an American jazz double-bassist, active principally as a sideman for white jazz musicians in the 1930s and 1940s.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Sid Weiss, much later, after I rejoined Benny for the "Seven Lively Arts Show" and then after that did some dates on the road. And Sid Weiss was on bass with Red Norvo and Slam Stewart.

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Sid Weiss was a good bass player... The band was really swinging

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Rodger Ray was not there because Sid Weiss had pulled a trick on him... Sid promised Rodger that he would take charge of the orchestra until I arrived

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: I was doing some stuff with Sid Weiss and Dorsey Terry. We were doing some rock and roll things for different publishers

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: Sid Weiss, the bass player that worked with Benny Goodman on the old band, an old timer, he told me that...
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: When I was substituting for Sid Weiss or Jack either Lesberg or somebody.
